text: Kesiakpa abha ekusi…Ы bat…ФћГ k…Ыtakpu k…Ыl…Ы beekusi…Ы beshogoћА n…Ы Apalete…Ы shu n…ЫћА ag…Ы y…Ыasara b…Фb…Ф …Фk…Ф…Ф,
translated: Kesiakpa said that the woman who was cooking had gone to the market and left the food on the fire, so Apalete went and added more firewood to it.

ref: вАЬWho are you?вАЭ Saul asked. вАЬI am Jesus,вАЭ the Lord answered. вАЬI am the one you are so cruel to.

translated: Saulo asked him, "Apalete, where are you going?" EbuiliuluћГ answered him, "I am going to see Jesus, the one who has called me."

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.

translated: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy and began to contradict what Paul was saying and insult him. Then Paul and Barnabas spoke boldly and said, "It was necessary that the word of God be spoken to you first. But since you reject it and consider yourselves unworthy of eternal life, we now turn to the Gentiles.

text: W…ФbodziћА an…Ф g…Ы …Фb…Ф…Ц…Фa…Ц…Ф …Фdal…Ы y…Ыishu …Фk…Ф banun…Ф petee n…Ы k…Ыg…Ы …Фm…Ф p…Ы oluћГ…Ф shu.
